name: Anki Vector
short_description: A small expressive home companion robot with a cloud voice assistant, a camera, and an animated face that lives on a desk and reacts to people around it.
long_description: Vector is a palm-sized desktop robot from Anki (the studio behind the earlier Cozmo) that pairs Pixar-style character animation with practical AI: a Qualcomm processor, an HD camera, a time-of-flight laser, cliff and touch sensors, and a four-microphone array let it map and roam a tabletop via SLAM, recognize faces and voices, answer questions, and emote through a high-resolution color 'eye' display. It is a clear case of physical AI as social character rather than tool: most of its design effort goes into legible, affective behavior so it reads as a tiny living companion. Anki shut down in April 2019 after failing to secure funding; later that year its assets, including Vector and Cozmo, were acquired by Digital Dream Labs, which kept the cloud servers running and shipped a Vector 2.0.

images.caption: An Anki Vector home companion robot on a desk.
images.alt_text: A small dark grey treaded robot with an illuminated rectangular blue-eyed face display, sitting on a wooden surface.

sources.title: Vector - ROBOTS: Your Guide to the World of Robotics
